_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.

preauth.label: Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers with treatment given details.

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment submitted?
Was the EMG, Nerve conduction velocity report of patient submitted?
Was the CT Angiography studies/MRI/CT for spine report of patient submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Was Detailed Indoor case papers with vital (BP and Pulse) and Treatment details submitted?
Was the Detailed Discharge Summary submitted with the date of the follow-up mentioned?

common_queries: Clinical notes or diagnostic reports (NCV/CT Angiography/EMG/MRI/CT Spine) not suggestive of neuromuscular disorder.
Clinical Notes, including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, or planned line of treatment, missing or incomplete at pre-authorization.
Creatine Phosphokinase (CPK) or Electrolytes report missing or incomplete at pre-authorization.
EMG studies or Nerve conduction velocity report missing or incomplete at pre-authorization.
Detailed Indoor case papers with vital (BP and Pulse) and treatment details missing or incomplete at claim stage.
Detailed Discharge Summary missing or without follow-up date at claim stage.

additional_information.min_doctor_qualification_desirable: DNB / MD (General Medicine / Pediatric Medicine); DM in Pediatric Neurology
clinical_key_pointers: Progressive acquired or hereditary neuromuscular diseases (NMDs) are disorders caused by an abnormality of any component of the lower motor neuron - anterior horn cell, peripheral nerve, neuromuscular junction (pre-synaptic or post-synaptic region), or muscle.
